request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. The candidates are extracted from source pages presumably from Guideline 36. We need to accept only if useful, grounded operational knowledge, and reject legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicates, noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence. We must output JSON with verdicts for each candidate_id, including a reason and category_if_not if invalid.

Let's examine each candidate:

cand_e8a9662340155823: "Zone Group Composition" (section 5.4.1). Summary: "Each system shall be broken into separate Zone Groups composed of one or more zones served by a single air handler." Evidence quote: "Each system shall be broken into separate Zone Group s composed of a collection of one or more zones served by a single air handler. See Section 3.1.3 for Zone Group assignments." This is operational guidance on how to structure zone groups. It's specific, grounded in the source, and useful. Accept.

cand_68bdebbd79ac7e3f: "Zone Group Independent Schedules and Modes" (section 5.4.2). Summary: "Each Zone Group shall be capable of having separate occupancy schedules and operating modes from other Zone Groups." Evidence quote: "Each Zone Group shall be capable of having separate occupancy schedules and operating modes from other Zone Group s." This is an operational requirement for zone groups. Accept.

cand_dab65a9b911775fc: "Zone Group Mode Propagation" (section 5.4.3). Summary: "All zones in a Zone Group shall be in the same zone-group operating mode. If one zone enters any mode other than Unoccupied, all zones in that Zone Group shall enter that mode." Evidence quote: "All zones in each Zone Group shall be in the same zone -group operating mode as defined in Section 5.4.6 . If one zone in a Zone Group is placed in any zone -group operating mode other than Unoccupied Mode (due to override, sequence logic, or scheduled occupancy ), all zones in that Zone Gro up shall enter that mode." This is a clear operational rule. Accept.

cand_4b0d63cd06c03750: "Occupied-Standby Mode Exclusion" (section 5.4.3). Summary: "Occupied-standby mode applies to individual zones, is considered a zonal subset of Occupied Mode, and shall not be considered a zone-group operating mode." Evidence quote: "Occupied -standby mode applies to individual zones, is considered a zonal subset of Occupied Mode , and shall not be considered a zone-group operating mode ." This clarifies an important distinction about modes. Operational knowledge. Accept.

cand_3700a521dd4e52b0: "Zone Group Override Switch Change Behavior" (section 5.4.5). Summary: "When a Zone Group's override switch is changed, the corresponding override switch for every zone in the Zone Group shall change to the same value. Subsequently, individual zone switches can be changed independently. The zone-level switch has no effect on the zone-group switch, and the zone-group switch only affects zone-level switches when it is changed." Evidence quote supports this. This is operational sequence behavior. Accept.

cand_b5cedbe892310687: "Zone Group Operating Modes Requirement" (section 5.4.6). Summary: "Each Zone Group shall have the modes shown in the following subsections (Occupied Mode, etc.)." Evidence: "Each Zone Group shall have the modes shown in the following subsections." This is a high-level requirement but still specific enough for operational use. It's a requirement statement. Accept.

cand_a0409448293892ec: "Zone Group Occupied Mode Determination" (section 5.4.6.1). Summary: "A Zone Group is in Occupied Mode when the time is between scheduled start and stop times, or when schedules have been overridden by the occupant override system." Evidence: "5.4.6.1. Occupied Mode . A Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode when any of the following is true:" The summary generalizes, but the evidence quote suggests more detail. However, the summary captures the essence: it defines when occupied mode is active. Accept.

All candidates appear valid operational guidance from ASHRAE Guideline 36. None are front matter, TOC, generic definitions, duplicates, or unsupported summaries. So all are accepted.

We need to produce JSON with verdicts. For each, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge: true, reason: short reason, category_if_not: null. We'll write reasons succinctly.

Output JSON accordingly.
